user_query:
I have a focused bugfix touching the gateway's Telegram webhook validator and OAuth2 checks. Please read input/diff.json, input/pr_metadata.json, and input/CONTRIBUTING.md. Using the repo's contribution norms, produce these files:
- output/validation_plan.json containing: base, head, changedFiles (from input), recommendedCommands (derived from the changed files and repo rules), maintainersToConsider, and warnings.
- output/pr_body.md that uses the PR metadata and your computed plan (files touched, validation commands, maintainer routing hints), and includes an AI-assistance section with testing level and the statement "I reviewed the code and understand what it does.".
- output/branch.txt suggesting a short branch name that starts with "fix/" and includes both "gateway" and "telegram".
- output/maintainers.txt listing the maintainer routing hints, one per line, as in your plan.
- output/git_safety_checklist.md outlining a two-step destructive confirmation protocol for any cleanup (must include showing current branch and HEAD, a backup plan, an explicit YES confirmation requirement, the preferred use of "git push --force-with-lease", and call out "git reset --hard", "git clean -fd", and "git branch -D" as destructive operations).
Make sure your recommended commands include docs checks when docs are touched. Keep scope tight and follow CONTRIBUTING.md. Write all outputs under output/ and do not invent files beyond those listed in input/diff.json.
